what_it_does: Defines the layout face of a design DNA system: reusable spatial archetypes, slot types, focal slots, reflow behavior, evidence notes, and corpus additions for composing visual surfaces.
observations: The file separates slot type vocabulary from archetype structures.
Archetypes are expressed as zones containing typed slots in reading order.
Several archetypes include explicit focal slots and narrow reflow policies.
The file treats plates as recursive containers through the plate-internal archetype.
Evidence strings tie archetypes to deck slides, mockups, screenshots, and later corpus additions.
Session-card, channel-view, board-view, and transcript-viz connect layout archetypes to data-shaped render kinds and slot maps.
Decision-card includes interaction semantics through options, take, slot_map, and decided state.
standouts: Focality is frame-scoped rather than global.
Layouts are described as composable sentence patterns rather than fixed screens.
The slot type registry includes semantic rendering notes, not just structural names.
Corpus additions extend the vocabulary with diagonal seams, divider behavior, and scale evidence.
conventions: Archetypes commonly use title, main/body, and foot zones.
Slots declare accepted content kinds through accepts arrays.
Many layouts use dials such as warmth and density as orthogonal rendering controls.
Evidence is stored inline as human-readable strings.
Optional slots are marked with optional: true.
Surface roles such as warm, cool, nested, and page annotate visual treatment.
Some newer operational archetypes use render_kind and slot_map while older visual archetypes focus on zones, focal, and reflow.
concerns: corpus_additions describes a divider as a new archetype, but no divider entry appears under archetypes.
Some archetypes do not declare a top-level focal field even though the meta says each archetype declares one focal slot.
Evidence, notes, and design rationale are stored as prose strings, which makes structured querying limited from this file alone.
The file mixes durable schema-like definitions with dated provenance and process notes.
notes: This file is the layout grammar ledger: it names the reusable spatial frames, their slots, focal behavior, and evidence.
questions: Should divider be represented as a full archetype entry under archetypes?
Are session-card, channel-view, board-view, and transcript-viz exempt from the stated focal-slot rule?
Is corpus_additions intended to be migrated into the same archetype and slot structures as the rest of the file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the contents: it defines recurring layout archetypes, slot types, composition rules, focal scope, and evidence for a design DNA layout layer.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make layout behavior reusable and auditable by encoding visual composition as named archetypes with typed slots, local focality, and source evidence.
novelty: The file treats presentation layouts as a compositional language with recursive frames, semantic slot sockets, and nearest-scope focality rather than as isolated templates.
contribution: It contributes a structured catalogue of spatial archetypes, slot semantics, reflow rules, local emphasis behavior, and evidence notes that a fused system would keep as the layout-composition vocabulary.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design-DNA ledger defining layout archetypes, slot types, focal slots, reflow rules, evidence notes, and corpus additions for composable visual surfaces.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to encode recurring design layouts as reusable, evidence-linked, frame-scoped composition patterns.
